result.summary: The page mostly renders as a standard casino lobby, but there is one clear data/content bug (a negative jackpot value) plus several layout truncation issues on the right edge and around the bottom navigation bar.

issues.description: The 'Cop The Lot Megaways' game tile displays a negative jackpot amount of '-$6,266.13', which is impossible for a jackpot value and indicates a data or formatting bug.
issues.suggestion: Fix the jackpot feed/parsing logic so negative values are never displayed; validate and clamp jackpot amounts to non-negative values, or hide the amount if the data is invalid.

issues.description: The bottom sticky navigation bar overlaps and cuts off the last row of game tiles ('Who Wants To Be A Millionaire', 'Rakin Bacon', 'Dark Waters'), obscuring their titles and content.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding/margin to the scrollable content area equal to the height of the sticky nav so tiles are not obscured.

issues.description: The top category nav label 'Hot Gar...' (Hot Games) is truncated at the right edge of the viewport.
issues.suggestion: If this is a horizontal carousel, add a fade/scroll affordance so partially visible items clearly indicate scrollability; otherwise adjust chip widths to avoid mid-word truncation.

issues.description: Game tiles in the right column ('Magnificent Pho...', 'Munchie Mil...', 'Hyperc...', 'Dark Waters') are cut off at the screen edge with truncated artwork/titles.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the game grid/carousel uses proper responsive sizing or peek styling so partially visible cards look intentional rather than clipped.

issues.description: The 'Casino' item in the bottom nav is styled as an underlined text link and lacks an icon-highlight state consistent with the other tabs, making the active state visually inconsistent.
issues.suggestion: Use a consistent active-state treatment (e.g., colored icon + label) across all bottom navigation items.
